description: Software developer managing a personal AI project, a freelance client engagement, and a language learning goal
persona: Mid-level full-stack engineer with 5 years experience. Personal project is an open-source AI memory layer. Freelance client is a data analytics company. Also learning Rust on the side.

memories.content: Working on OpenMemory, a personal AI memory layer that stores user context in a graph on decentralized infrastructure.
memories.label: OpenMemory project

memories.content: Starting a freelance contract with Veritas Analytics to build a data pipeline for their customer segmentation system.
memories.label: Veritas Analytics contract

memories.content: Learning Rust from The Book - completed chapters 1 through 4 on ownership and borrowing. Ownership model is clicking.
memories.label: Rust learning: ownership and borrowing

memories.content: The Veritas project requires integration with their internal Kafka cluster. Need to study Kafka consumer groups and partition lag.
memories.label: Kafka integration for Veritas

memories.content: Implemented BFS traversal for OpenMemory graph retrieval. Nodes with more co-activations rank higher as context seeds.
memories.label: OpenMemory BFS retrieval implemented

memories.content: Rust lifetimes are still confusing. Watched Jon Gjengset's video twice. Smart pointers (Box, Rc, RefCell) starting to make sense.
memories.label: Rust: lifetimes and smart pointers

memories.content: Veritas asked for an accelerated timeline - data pipeline needs to be production-ready in 6 weeks instead of 10. Stressful request.
memories.label: Veritas deadline accelerated

memories.content: Started implementing document ingestion for OpenMemory. Users can upload PDFs and Markdown files and have them chunked into the memory graph.
memories.label: OpenMemory document ingestion started

memories.content: Kafka consumer group rebalancing is causing lag spikes in the Veritas pipeline. Added max.poll.interval.ms tuning and increased session timeout.
memories.label: Veritas Kafka lag spike debugging

memories.content: DocumentChunkerService implemented for OpenMemory - splits text into semantic chunks of max 1500 characters at paragraph boundaries.
memories.label: DocumentChunkerService implemented

memories.content: Difficult conversation with the Veritas client about scope creep - they want real-time dashboards added to the original fixed-price quote.
memories.label: Veritas scope creep conversation

memories.content: Veritas pipeline passing all integration tests against the staging Kafka cluster. Preparing staging deployment next week.
memories.label: Veritas integration tests passing

memories.content: Designed the OpenMemory benchmark: compare recency-flat, graph-guided, and goal-biased retrieval on synthetic corpora with LLM-as-judge scoring.
memories.label: OpenMemory benchmark design

memories.content: Rust async story with Tokio is complex - started the async Rust book. Expect it will take another month before it feels natural.
memories.label: Rust async and Tokio learning

memories.content: Veritas staging deployment succeeded. Client review call scheduled for Thursday. Final invoice submitted after signoff.
memories.label: Veritas staging deployment succeeded

memories.content: Goal-biased retrieval implemented in OpenMemory. Goal nodes now seed BFS before weight-ranked candidates, biasing context toward active work.
memories.label: OpenMemory goal-biased retrieval shipped

memories.content: Sent Veritas the final invoice draft - 18,400 for the full engagement. Waiting for approval before closing the contract.
memories.label: Veritas invoice sent

memories.content: Reading about actor model concurrency in Rust. Considering Actix-web for a future API project.
memories.label: Rust: actor model and Actix-web

memories.content: OpenMemory benchmark harness implementation started. Synthetic corpora, three retrieval strategies, LLM-as-judge scoring, CSV and Markdown output.
memories.label: OpenMemory benchmark harness started

goals.content: Goal: ship the OpenMemory benchmark harness and produce a retrieval quality measurement before end of April.
goals.label: Ship OpenMemory benchmark

goals.content: Goal: close the Veritas Analytics engagement cleanly - invoice approved, payment received, contract signed off.
goals.label: Close Veritas contract

goals.content: Goal: complete The Rust Book and build one working Rust project before summer.
goals.label: Rust proficiency by summer

questions.question: What is the current status of the Veritas Analytics project and what still needs to happen?
What should I focus on for OpenMemory this week?
How is my Rust learning progressing and what am I working on next?
What are my most time-sensitive items right now?
What do I know about distributed systems from my recent work?
